sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.11 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: Let$F H$be free groups such that there exists a free group$G$of finite rank with$F G H$, and let$r$be the least of the ranks of such groups$G$. Which, if any, of the following statements must hold? [r]{(i)\,} There is a largest$G$of rank$r$between$F$and$H$. [r]{(i$'$)\,} For any two$G_1$,$G_2$of rank$r$between$F$and$H$, the subgroup$G_1, G_2$has rank$r$. [r]{(ii)\,} There is a smallest$G$of rank$r...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
